content.question: The net causal effect of early selection (6/8-year grammar-school entry at age 10–12) on individual student achievement and social mobility is not directly measured in Hungarian evidence; test-score gaps at selection reflect composition, not school value-added.
content.reduction_path: Quasi-experimental studies (e.g., discontinuity design at score cutoffs, matched-pairs within-municipality comparison controlling for family background) comparing life outcomes of grammar-track vs. non-grammar track entrants with similar pre-entry test scores.
